name: Tropical Stuffed Cornish Hens
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove giblet packet from hens; rinse hens with cool water. Drain and set aside.
2. Drain pineapple well, reserving juice. Place the juice in a small saucepan with jelly.
3. Heat over low heat until jelly melts, stirring occasionally.
4. Cook onions in butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat until tender.
5. Add rice, reserved pineapple, currants, nuts, broth, and ginger; heat thoroughly.
6. Spoon about 2/3 cup mixture into the cavity of each hen. Use wooden picks, soaked in water, to enclose filling.
7. Place hens breast side up in a roasting pan on a rack coated with cooking spray.
8. Baste with jelly mixture. Roast at 400 degrees F. for 10 minutes; reduce oven temperature to 350 degrees F.
9. Continue roasting for 1 hour or until internal temperature registers 180 to 185 degrees F., basting every 30 minutes with jelly mixture.
10. Cover remaining stuffing and bake during the last 30 minutes with the remaining rice.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.
Substitute red currant jelly for red pepper jelly, if desired.
